# @alunsoldgroup/bug-reporter

Bug reporting UI and adapters for developer-ready tickets.

Package:

```sh
npm install @alunsoldgroup/bug-reporter
```

Repository:

https://github.com/alunsoldantarctica/bug-reporter

Demo:

https://alunsoldantarctica.github.io/bug-reporter/

## What This Package Does

It helps web apps collect high-signal bug reports from staff, QA, or trusted
users. Reports can include:

- page URL and surface
- CSS selector for the affected element
- selected element text/HTML/viewport metadata
- screenshots
- optional microphone narration
- optional PostHog replay session id
- optional Linear issue creation through a server adapter
- optional Claude Code ticket enrichment workflow

## Best Fit

Use this when integrating an in-app bug reporter into:

- React apps
- Astro apps
- Cloudflare Workers deployments
- admin panels
- staff-only public-site overlays
- QA/beta feedback flows
- Linear-based engineering workflows

## Security Boundary

Never expose Linear, PostHog personal API, or AI Gateway secrets in browser code.

Use browser code only to collect report context. Use a server route to:

- authenticate staff/admin users
- redact sensitive data
- create Linear issues
- upload attachments
- transcribe audio
- format PostHog replay URLs

## Quick Integration

Client:

```ts
import { createHttpBugReportAdapter } from "@alunsoldgroup/bug-reporter";

const bugReporter = createHttpBugReportAdapter("/api/bug-report");

await bugReporter.submit({
  source: "site",
  surface: window.location.pathname,
  url: window.location.href,
  message: "Submit button stays disabled after changing dates.",
  severity: "medium",
  userAgent: navigator.userAgent,
  replaySessionId: window.posthog?.get_session_id?.(),
});
```

Astro on Cloudflare Workers:

```ts
import type { APIRoute } from "astro";
import { createAstroCloudflareBugReportHandler } from "@alunsoldgroup/bug-reporter";

export const prerender = false;

export const POST: APIRoute = async ({ request, locals }) => {
  const handle = createAstroCloudflareBugReportHandler({
    env: locals.runtime.env,
    requireAuth: (req) => Boolean(req.headers.get("cookie")?.includes("staff_session=")),
  });

  return handle(request);
};
```

## Useful Files

- `README.md` — full install and integration docs
- `docs/demo/index.html` — static GitHub Pages demo
- `examples/astro-cloudflare/src/pages/api/bug-report.ts` — Astro route example
- `examples/claude-code/linear-bug-context-routine.md` — routine for enriching Linear tickets
- `src/index.ts` — core client adapter, types, audio helpers
- `src/adapters/astro-cloudflare.ts` — server adapter for Astro on Cloudflare Workers

## Agent Instructions

When asked to integrate this package:

1. Install `@alunsoldgroup/bug-reporter`.
2. Add a server route. Do not call Linear or AI Gateway from browser code.
3. Gate the route with staff/admin auth.
4. Add the client reporter UI in the host app.
5. If PostHog is present, pass `window.posthog.get_session_id()`.
6. If voice is enabled, keep it opt-in and disclose recording.
7. Add a Linear webhook or scheduled routine to run the Claude Code enrichment workflow.
8. Verify with one mock report and one real authenticated report.
